title: Tree styling: row height, selection bar, indent guides
description:
## Goal

Restyle the generic Tree component (packages/ui) to match editor-quality density and selection behavior: 22px rows, full-width selection bar, and indent guides.

## Behaviors

- Tree rows are 22px tall (min-height: 22px, tight padding)
- Selected items use a full-width background fill with no border-radius — background spans the entire sidebar width
- Focused items use a subtle background instead of an outline
- Hovered items keep current behavior (bg-surface-hover)
- Indent guides render as subtle vertical lines at each nesting depth via ::before pseudo-element
- Indentation uses --tree-item-level CSS variable on the chevron's padding-left, not on the row
- A new Tree.Indent sub-component provides a spacer for leaf items (no chevron) to align with folder rows
- --gr-tree-indent custom property (20px) controls indent step size

## Success criteria

- [ ] .item has min-height: 22px with reduced vertical padding
- [ ] .item has no border-radius
- [ ] .item[data-focused] uses background instead of outline
- [ ] .item::before draws vertical guide lines using repeating-linear-gradient
- [ ] .chevron padding-left uses calc((var(--tree-item-level, 1) - 1) * var(--gr-tree-indent))
- [ ] Tree.Indent renders a span sized by --tree-item-level for leaf item alignment
- [ ] Existing Tree.test.tsx tests pass
- [ ] New tests verify indent and styling classes
- [ ] Tree.preview.tsx visually confirms all changes

title: Editor-quality file tree sidebar
description: ## Context The file tree sidebar is functional but visually basic compared to VSCode/JetBrains. It lacks file icons, indent guides, compact folders, and uses an outline-based selection instead of a full-width background fill. This epic brings it to editor-quality through 7 visual improvements in 3 phases. ## Requirements - [ ] Tree rows use 22px height matching VSCode density - [ ] Selected/focused items use full-width background fill instead of outline — no border-radius, background spans edge-to-edge - [ ] Indent guides render as subtle vertical lines at each nesting depth using --tree-item-level CSS variable - [ ] Indentation lives on the chevron/spacer (inner element), never on the TreeItem row itself - [ ] File type icons render next to each filename via vscode-material-icons (910 icons, rendering) - [ ] Folder icons show open/closed state matching expand/collapse - [ ] Leaf items align with folder items via an Indent spacer where the chevron would be - [ ] Filename text color reflects change type: green (added), red (deleted), yellow (modified), muted (renamed) - [ ] Single-child directory chains flatten into one row (compact folders), using deepest dir path as React Aria id - [ ] Directories inherit the highest-priority changeType from descendants (added > deleted > modified > renamed) - [ ] Directory rows show colored text but no letter badge ## Success criteria 1. All 7 visual improvements render correctly in both Catppuccin Latte and Mocha themes 2. Existing Tree and FileTree tests pass without modification (new tests added alongside) 3. File icons load via tags from static assets — no dangerouslySetInnerHTML 4. Compact folders produce correct tree shape: `src/events` as one row when `events` has a single child directory 5. Directory status propagation colors parent folders based on child file statuses 6. pnpm test, pnpm typecheck, pnpm lint all pass ## Anti-patterns - Do NOT put level-based padding on the TreeItem row — only on inner elements (chevron, spacer). This breaks full-width selection highlight. - Do NOT use dangerouslySetInnerHTML for icons — use with vscode-material-icons URL API - Do NOT compute compact folders or status propagation at render time — do it in the data layer (buildFileTree) - Do NOT show letter badges (M, A, D, R) on directory rows — only colored text - Do NOT add keyboard shortcuts in this epic — UI visual quality only - Do NOT modify the Tree UI component's public API surface beyond adding Indent sub-component ## Approach Three phases, each a separate PR: **Phase 1 — Tree styling (CSS-only):** Tighter row height, full-width selection bar, indent guides + chevron indentation. All in packages/ui Tree component. **Phase 2 — File icons + colors:** New FileIcon component in packages/ui using vscode-material-icons. Integrate into FileTree with TreeItemContent render function for isExpanded access. Add change type color classes to filename text. **Phase 3 — Data transforms:** compactTree() and propagateChangeType() functions in useFileList.ts. Pure data-layer transforms called inside buildFileTree after sortNodes. ## Design decisions | Decision | Choice | Rationale | |---|---|---| | Icon library | vscode-material-icons | Folder open/closed support, 910 icons, rendering vs dangerouslySetInnerHTML | | Indentation | Chevron padding-left | React Aria pattern — row stays full-width for selection highlight | | Compact folder identity | Deepest dir path | Stable React Aria id for selection/expansion | | Status propagation | Data layer (buildFileTree) | Pure function, testable, no render-time computation | | Row height | 22px (VSCode standard) | Editor-density familiarity | | Deleted propagation | Yes (unlike VSCode) | Code review tool — deleted files matter for review visibility |

title: Create Shiki Web Worker with message protocol
description:
## Goal

Create a dedicated Web Worker that owns the Shiki highlighter instance and responds to highlight requests via postMessage. Define the message protocol types shared between main thread and worker.

## Behaviors

1. Worker initializes Shiki highlighter lazily on first highlight request
2. Worker loads language grammars on demand per request, not all at init
3. Worker receives lines with keys and returns ThemedToken arrays keyed by line key
4. Worker caches highlighted results by {filePath, lineKey} to skip re-work
5. Worker falls back to plaintext tokens for unknown languages
6. Shared TypeScript types define the request/response message protocol

## Success criteria

- [ ] Worker file uses Vite's native `new Worker(new URL(...), { type: 'module' })` pattern
- [ ] Message protocol types are importable from both worker and main thread code
- [ ] Language grammars are loaded lazily, not bundled at init
- [ ] Cache prevents re-highlighting the same line for the same file
- [ ] Unknown languages produce plain text tokens instead of errors
- [ ] No integration with DiffViewer yet (next task)
design:
## Expanded plan

### New files

1. `packages/web/src/workers/highlightProtocol.ts` — Shared message types
2. `packages/web/src/workers/highlightEngine.ts` — Core highlighting logic (testable without worker)
3. `packages/web/src/workers/highlightEngine.test.ts` — Tests for engine
4. `packages/web/src/workers/highlightWorker.ts` — Thin worker entry point wiring postMessage to engine

### Message protocol (highlightProtocol.ts)

- HighlightRequest: { type, filePath, language, theme, lines: {key, content}[] }
- HighlightResponse: { type, filePath, tokens: Record<string, {content, color?}[]> }
- WorkerRequest = HighlightRequest; WorkerResponse = HighlightResponse

### Engine logic (highlightEngine.ts)

Exports handleHighlightRequest(request): Promise<HighlightResponse>

State: lazy highlighterPromise, loadedLanguages Set, cache Map<filePath:lineKey, token[]>

Flow:
1. Init highlighter lazily on first call (themes only, no langs)
2. Load language on demand via highlighter.loadLanguage(). On error, fall back to plaintext
3. Filter out cached lines. Join uncached lines, call codeToTokens once (preserves grammar state). Split back by index. Serialize ThemedToken to {content, color}. Store in cache
4. Return response with all tokens (cached + fresh)

### Worker entry (highlightWorker.ts)

Thin: imports handleHighlightRequest, wires self.onmessage to it, posts response back

### TDD cycles

1. Protocol types compile — import and use types in test
2. Returns tokens for lines — mock shiki, 2 lines in, token map with both keys out
3. Lazy init — createHighlighter not called until first request
4. Language on demand — request with python triggers loadLanguage
5. Cache hit — same request twice, codeToTokens called once
6. Unknown language fallback — fakeLang rejected, plaintext tokens returned

title: Editor shell polish
description:
## Context

Greppa's UI should feel like a code editor, not a web app. The current shell has multiple headers (app header, diff header, detail panel tabs), 1px borders everywhere, and a status bar that only shows review progress. This epic consolidates the shell into a cohesive editor-like experience.

## Requirements

1. **Single unified header** — merge the app header (logo, theme picker) and diff file header (file path, change badge, branch indicator) into one header bar. The detail panel tab bar remains as panel-internal navigation.
2. **Tonal separation over borders** — remove all 1px solid borders (header, status bar, detail panel tabs/threads/info rows, diff divider, panel separators). Use background shade differences (--gr-color-bg-subtle vs --gr-color-bg-surface vs --gr-color-bg-default) to distinguish regions.
3. **Editor-style status bar** — left side: review progress, branch. Right side: file-local metadata (language, encoding, line/col position). Requires adding file metadata to StatusBar props.
4. **Document no-border principle in VISION.md** — add a design principle about preferring tonal layering over structural borders.

## Anti-patterns

- Do not add new borders to replace removed ones.
- Do not introduce Tailwind — the project uses CSS modules with --gr-* custom properties.
- Do not change component behavior or data flow — this is purely visual/structural.

## Success criteria

- Zero 1px solid borders in the app shell (header, status bar, panel separators). Exception: the active tab indicator in DetailPanel may keep its bottom accent.
- One header bar visible at the top of the app.
- StatusBar shows file metadata on the right when a file is selected.
- All panels visually distinct through background shade differences alone.
- VISION.md contains the no-border design principle.
- All existing tests pass.

title: Add no-border design principle to VISION.md
description:
## Goal

Add a design principle to docs/VISION.md under "Design principles" stating that the UI prefers tonal layering (background shade shifts) over structural borders (1px solid lines) to separate regions.

## Why

Sets the ground rule before the visual changes in subsequent tasks. Other tasks in this epic reference this principle.

## Behaviors

- VISION.md design principles section contains a new bullet about tonal separation over borders.

## Success criteria

- New bullet point in the Design principles section of VISION.md.
- Principle explains tonal layering: use --gr-color-bg-subtle, --gr-color-bg-surface, --gr-color-bg-default shade differences rather than borders.
- Concise — one bullet, 2-3 sentences max.

title: Remove structural borders and use tonal separation
description:
## Goal

Replace all 1px solid borders in the app shell with background shade differences, implementing the tonal separation principle from VISION.md.

## Why

The no-border design principle (task .1) is documented. This task applies it across the UI so all subsequent tasks (unified header, status bar) build on a border-free shell.

## Scope

Every `border` declaration in the app shell CSS modules except:
- The active tab indicator (`border-bottom: 2px solid` on `Tabs .trigger[data-state='active']`) — this is an accent, not a structural border.
- `border-radius` — not structural.
- `border: none` / `border-bottom: none` resets — harmless.
- The `Separator` in App.module.css — this is a resize handle, uses `background` not `border`, keep as-is.

### Borders to remove

| File | Selector | Declaration | Replacement |
|------|----------|-------------|-------------|
| Header.module.css:6 | `.header` | `border-bottom: 1px solid var(--gr-color-border-default)` | Remove. Header already has `background: var(--gr-color-bg-subtle)` which contrasts with body `--gr-color-bg-default`. |
| Header.module.css:19 | `.themePicker` | `border: 1px solid var(--gr-color-border-default)` | Remove. Use `background: var(--gr-color-bg-surface)` contrast against header's `--gr-color-bg-subtle`. |
| StatusBar.module.css:5 | `.statusBar` | `border-top: 1px solid var(--gr-color-border-default)` | Remove. StatusBar already has `background: var(--gr-color-bg-subtle)`. |
| DiffViewer.module.css:18 | `.fileHeader` | `border-bottom: 1px solid var(--gr-color-border-muted)` | Remove. fileHeader has `background: var(--gr-color-bg-subtle)`, diff content uses default bg. |
| DiffViewer.module.css:36 | `.side[data-side='right']` | `border-left: 1px solid var(--gr-color-border-default)` | Remove. Left/right sides are already visually distinct via diff coloring. |
| DetailPanel.module.css:10 | `.thread` | `border-bottom: 1px solid var(--gr-color-border-muted)` | Remove border-bottom. Add `margin-bottom: var(--gr-space-sm)` gap + thread items already have `background: var(--gr-color-bg-surface)` on `.comment`. |
| DetailPanel.module.css:71 | `.infoRow` | `border-bottom: 1px solid var(--gr-color-border-muted)` | Remove border-bottom. Rely on row padding for visual separation. |
| Tabs.module.css:3 (packages/ui) | `.list` | `border-bottom: 1px solid var(--gr-color-border-default)` | Remove. Tab list has `background: var(--gr-color-bg-subtle)` contrasting with content area. |

## Behaviors

- No `1px solid` borders remain in app shell CSS (Header, StatusBar, DiffViewer, DetailPanel, Tabs) except the active tab accent.
- Regions remain visually distinct through background shade layering.
- Thread and infoRow separators use spacing instead of lines.

## Success criteria

- Zero `1px solid` border declarations in the listed CSS modules (excluding tab accent and border-radius).
- Visual separation achieved through existing `--gr-color-bg-*` variables.
- All existing tests pass.
- No new CSS custom properties introduced.

title: Merge app header and file header into single unified header
description:
## Goal

Merge the app Header (logo + theme picker) and the DiffViewer fileHeader (change badge + file path + old path) into a single top-level header bar.

## Why

Epic requirement 1: "Single unified header." Currently there are two header bars — the app header at the very top and the sticky file header inside DiffViewer. Merging them reduces visual clutter and matches the editor metaphor (one title bar).

## Scope

### Header component changes
- Accept optional props for the currently selected file: filePath, oldPath, changeType (from DiffFile).
- Render left: logo. Center/left-of-center: change badge + file path + old path (when a file is selected). Right: theme picker.
- When no file is selected, center area is empty or shows a subtle placeholder.

### DiffViewer changes
- Remove the .fileHeader div and its CSS entirely.
- DiffViewer no longer renders file path or change badge — that moves to Header.

### App.tsx changes
- Pass selectedDiff metadata (path, oldPath, changeType) to Header.
- No change to DiffViewer props — it still receives the full diff for rendering hunks.

### CSS changes
- Header.module.css: add styles for the file path area (use --gr-font-mono for paths).
- DiffViewer.module.css: remove .fileHeader rule.
- No borders. Tonal separation only.

## Behaviors

- Single header bar visible at top containing logo, file info, and theme picker.
- File info updates when a different file is selected.
- No file info shown when no file is selected.

## Success criteria

- No .fileHeader in DiffViewer.
- Header shows file path and change badge when a file is selected.

## Goal

Extend StatusBar to show file-local metadata on the right side when a file is selected: language and encoding.

## Why

Epic requirement 3: "Editor-style status bar — left side: review progress, branch. Right side: file-local metadata (language, encoding, line/col position)." This makes the status bar match the editor metaphor (VSCode shows language, encoding, line info in its status bar).

## Scope

### FileInfo type changes (fixtures/types.ts)

- Add encoding: string (e.g. "UTF-8") to FileInfo interface.
- Update fixture data (fileInfo.json) with language and encoding values.

### StatusBar component changes
- Add optional props: language (string | null), encoding (string | null).
- Left side: review progress (existing).
- Right side: language + encoding, shown only when values are non-null.
- Use justify-content: space-between to push left/right apart.

### StatusBar.module.css changes
- Add right-side segment styles matching the existing progress style (same font, size, color).

### App.tsx changes
- Pass selectedFileInfo?.language and selectedFileInfo?.encoding to StatusBar.

### Test updates
- Update StatusBar tests to cover file metadata rendering.
- Update fixture data references if needed.

## Behaviors

- When a file is selected, status bar right side shows "TypeScript  UTF-8" (language and encoding separated by spacing).
- When no file is selected, right side is empty.
- Left side continues showing review progress unchanged.

## Success criteria

- StatusBar renders file language and encoding on the right when a file is selected.
- StatusBar right side is empty when no file is selected.
- FileInfo type includes language and encoding fields.
- All existing tests pass.

title: Editor-quality file tree sidebar
description: ## Context The file tree sidebar is functional but visually basic compared to VSCode/JetBrains. It lacks file icons, indent guides, compact folders, and uses an outline-based selection instead of a full-width background fill. ## Design summary Bring the file tree to editor-quality through 7 visual improvements in 3 phases: ### Phase 1: Tree styling (CSS-only) 1. **Tighter row height** — 22px rows matching VSCode density 2. **Full-width selection bar** — replace orange outline with background fill spanning the entire sidebar width. Key: never put level-based padding on TreeItem — only on inner elements (chevron/spacer) 3. **Indent guides** — subtle vertical lines via ::before pseudo-element using --tree-item-level CSS variable and repeating-linear-gradient ### Phase 2: File icons + change type colors 4. **File/folder icons** — vscode-material-icons (910 icons, folder open/closed pairs, rendering). New FileIcon component in packages/ui 5. **Change type colors on filename text** — color the label itself per status (green=added, red=deleted, yellow=modified, muted=renamed) using existing design tokens ### Phase 3: Data transforms 6. **Compact folders** — flatten single-child directory chains (VSCode's algorithm: loop-based merge, deepest dir path as identity) 7. **Directory status propagation** — folders inherit highest-priority changeType from descendants (added > deleted > modified > renamed) ## Key decisions | Decision | Choice | Rationale | |---|---|---| | Icon library | vscode-material-icons | Folder open/closed support, 910 icons, img rendering vs dangerouslySetInnerHTML | | Indentation | Chevron padding-left | React Aria pattern — row stays full-width for selection highlight | | Compact folder identity | Deepest dir path | Stable React Aria id for selection/expansion | | Status propagation | Data layer (buildFileTree) | Pure function, testable, no render-time computation | | Row height | 22px (VSCode standard) | Editor-density familiarity | ## Constraints - TreeItemContent render props provide: hasChildItems, isExpanded, level - Tree UI component is generic (packages/ui), FileTree is domain-specific (packages/web) - Must follow TDD, CSS modules, existing design token system - Deleted files DO propagate status (unlike VSCode — review tool needs this)
design:
## Context

The file tree sidebar is functional but visually basic compared to VSCode/JetBrains. It lacks file icons, indent guides, compact folders, and uses an outline-based selection instead of a full-width background fill.

## Design summary

Bring the file tree to editor-quality through 7 visual improvements in 3 phases:

### Phase 1: Tree styling (CSS-only)
1. **Tighter row height** — 22px rows matching VSCode density
2. **Full-width selection bar** — replace orange outline with background fill spanning the entire sidebar width. Key: never put level-based padding on TreeItem — only on inner elements (chevron/spacer)
3. **Indent guides** — subtle vertical lines via ::before pseudo-element using --tree-item-level CSS variable and repeating-linear-gradient

### Phase 2: File icons + change type colors
4. **File/folder icons** — vscode-material-icons (910 icons, folder open/closed pairs, img rendering). New FileIcon component in packages/ui
5. **Change type colors on filename text** — color the label itself per status (green=added, red=deleted, yellow=modified, muted=renamed) using existing design tokens

### Phase 3: Data transforms
6. **Compact folders** — flatten single-child directory chains (VSCode algorithm: loop-based merge, deepest dir path as identity)
7. **Directory status propagation** — folders inherit highest-priority changeType from descendants (added > deleted > modified > renamed)

## Key decisions

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| Icon library | vscode-material-icons | Folder open/closed support, 910 icons, img rendering vs dangerouslySetInnerHTML |
| Indentation | Chevron padding-left | React Aria pattern — row stays full-width for selection highlight |
| Compact folder identity | Deepest dir path | Stable React Aria id for selection/expansion |
| Status propagation | Data layer (buildFileTree) | Pure function, testable, no render-time computation |
| Row height | 22px (VSCode standard) | Editor-density familiarity |

## Constraints

- TreeItemContent render props provide: hasChildItems, isExpanded, level
- Tree UI component is generic (packages/ui), FileTree is domain-specific (packages/web)
- Must follow TDD, CSS modules, existing design token system
- Deleted files DO propagate status (unlike VSCode — review tool needs this)

title: File icons and change type colors
description: ## Goal Add file type icons via vscode-material-icons and change type color classes to filenames in the FileTree. ## Behaviors - FileIcon component in packages/ui renders tags from self-hosted vscode-material-icons SVGs - FileIcon accepts name (filename/dirname), isDirectory, and isExpanded props - Files show type-specific icons (typescript.svg, react_ts.svg, etc.) resolved via getIconForFilePath - Folders show open/closed icons: getIconForDirectoryPath returns e.g. folder-src, appending -open for expanded state - Filename text color reflects changeType: green (added), red (deleted), yellow (modified), muted (renamed) - Directories show colored text but no letter badge when they have a changeType - FileTree integrates FileIcon, passing isExpanded from TreeItemContent render props - Vite config copies vscode-material-icons SVGs to /material-icons/ in public output ## Success criteria - [ ] FileIcon component renders with correct src for files and directories - [ ] Folder icons toggle between closed and open variants based on isExpanded - [ ] Filename text uses change type color CSS classes - [ ] Directories with changeType show colored text, no Badge - [ ] Files with changeType show both colored text and Badge - [ ] tags load from static assets, no dangerouslySetInnerHTML - [ ] Existing FileTree tests pass - [ ] New tests cover FileIcon and color class rendering - [ ] pnpm test, pnpm typecheck, pnpm lint all pass

title: Caddy reverse proxy for local dev
description: Set up Caddy as a reverse proxy so each worktree gets predictable *.greppa.localhost domains with zero manual DNS config.

comments.author: Oskar Grunning
comments.text:
EPIC CONTRACT

## Requirements

1. Each worktree gets three domains resolved via *.localhost (RFC 6761, no /etc/hosts needed):
   - <name>.greppa.localhost -> web app (DEV_PORT)
   - <name>.greppa.localhost/api/* -> API server (API_PORT)
   - playground.<name>.greppa.localhost -> UI playground (PLAYGROUND_PORT)
   - Exception: main worktree uses greppa.localhost (no prefix)

2. worktreeEnv.ts writes a per-worktree Caddy snippet to ~/.config/caddy/greppa/<name>.caddy and runs caddy reload -- fully automated, no manual steps.

3. A root Caddyfile in the repo uses import ~/.config/caddy/greppa/*.caddy to aggregate all active worktrees.

4. Vite dev servers print the Caddy-mapped URL (e.g. https://greppa.localhost) alongside the normal localhost:port output.

5. Remove the Vite /api proxy from packages/web/vite.config.ts -- Caddy handles this now.

6. STACK.md updated to document the Caddy setup.

## Anti-patterns

- No dnsmasq, systemd-resolved, or /etc/hosts modifications
- No manual steps per worktree -- agents must be able to create worktrees and get working URLs automatically
- No hardcoded ports in the Caddyfile -- everything derives from .env.local / worktreeEnv.ts

## Success criteria

- pnpm dev in any worktree writes the Caddy snippet, reloads Caddy, and prints the mapped URLs
- Navigating to greppa.localhost in a browser reaches the web app
- Navigating to greppa.localhost/api/... reaches the API server
- Navigating to playground.greppa.localhost reaches the UI playground
- A second worktree gets its own subdomain without conflicting with the first
- No DNS configuration required on the host machine

title: Extend worktreeEnv to generate Caddy snippet and reload
description:
Extend scripts/worktreeEnv.ts to:

1. Add a buildCaddySnippet function that generates a Caddy config snippet for a single worktree. Takes worktree name, apiPort, devPort, playgroundPort. Produces the three domain blocks (web, /api handle, playground).

2. Write the snippet to ~/.config/caddy/greppa/<name>.caddy (mkdir -p the directory).

3. Write a root Caddyfile to ~/.config/caddy/greppa/Caddyfile that does import ./*.caddy (only if it does not exist yet).

4. Run caddy reload --config ~/.config/caddy/greppa/Caddyfile after writing the snippet. Fail gracefully if caddy is not installed or not running.

5. Add a CADDY_DOMAIN env var to .env.local output (e.g. CADDY_DOMAIN=greppa.localhost or CADDY_DOMAIN=feat-foo.greppa.localhost).

6. Export buildCaddySnippet and add tests for it in worktreeEnv.test.ts.

Domain scheme:
- main -> greppa.localhost, playground.greppa.localhost
- <other> -> <name>.greppa.localhost, playground.<name>.greppa.localhost

title: Remove Vite /api proxy and print Caddy-mapped URL
description:
1. Remove the proxy config from packages/web/vite.config.ts — Caddy handles /api routing now.

2. Add Caddy-mapped URL to Vite server output. Read CADDY_DOMAIN from env and print it alongside the normal localhost:port URL.

Covers epic requirements 4 and 5.

title: Document Caddy reverse proxy setup in STACK.md
description:
Add a section to docs/STACK.md documenting:
- The Caddy reverse proxy architecture (*.greppa.localhost domains)
- How worktreeEnv.ts auto-generates snippets
- The root Caddyfile import pattern
- How to install and start Caddy for local dev

Covers epic requirement 6.
